# Fivebar

> Five-bar linkage simulator, singularity visualisation, and configuration search tools for planar parallel robotics and mechanism design.

This repository explores a planar five-bar linkage with a rigid L5 tool extension. It is intended for research and early mechanism design, especially reachability, fixed-branch singularity analysis, condition number visualisation, and idealised quasi-static holding torque estimates.

## Primary Links

- Live project page: https://unswei.github.io/fivebar/
- Web simulator: https://unswei.github.io/fivebar/web/
- Singularity plot: https://unswei.github.io/fivebar/web/singularity.html
- Source repository: https://github.com/unswei/fivebar
- Lab website: https://unswei.github.io/
- Project page: https://unswei.github.io/projects/fivebar/

## Key Concepts

- Mechanism: two actuated base joints, two passive elbow joints, a closed-chain wrist, and a rigid L5 tool extension.
- Tool tip: the endpoint of L5; it is not the same point as the five-bar loop closure wrist.
- IK branch pair: the left and right open-chain elbow choices, written for example as (+1, -1).
- Closure branch: the wrist intersection choice after the two elbows are known. It is a separate binary assembly choice from the IK branch pair.
- Condition number: the ratio between the largest and smallest singular values of the linkage Jacobian. Values near 1 are well conditioned; high values indicate proximity to a weak or singular direction.
- Serial singularity: one side chain is folded or stretched so its base actuator loses useful leverage.
- Parallel singularity: the closed-chain constraints become locally dependent, reducing stiffness or creating an uncontrolled motion direction.

## Current Default Configuration

```toml
[linkage]
d = 0.1917
base_y = -0.3745
l1 = 0.5829
l2 = 0.4632
l3 = 0.3308
l4 = 0.7360
l5 = 0.3790
left_branch = 1
right_branch = -1

[simulator]
left_angle_deg = 15.534233824250094
right_angle_deg = 43.922888743202634
distance = 0.5
target_angle_deg = 90.0
closure_branch = -1
tip_mass_kg = 1.0
gravity_x = -9.81
gravity_y = 0.0
```

## Important Source Files

- README.md: human-readable overview, setup instructions, definitions, limitations, and citation guidance.
- index.html: static landing page for GitHub Pages.
- web/index.html: browser simulator UI.
- web/app.js: static JavaScript simulator logic.
- web/singularity.html: browser singularity visualisation UI.
- web/singularity.js: static JavaScript singularity sampler and plotter.
- web/styles.css: shared web styling.
- configs/default.toml: example runtime configuration shared by the Python tools.
- src/fivebar/config.py: Python configuration dataclasses and TOML loading.
- src/fivebar/simulator.py: interactive 2D Matplotlib simulator.
- src/fivebar/singularity_plotter.py: fixed-branch singularity plotter.
- src/fivebar/spatial_simulator.py: interactive 3D swept-linkage simulator.
- src/fivebar/spatial_reachability.py: 3D half-sphere reachability sampler.
- src/fivebar/search_config.py: coarse linkage configuration search.
- src/fivebar/search_torque_config.py: sampled torque-minimising configuration search.

## Running The Project

Install Python dependencies:

```bash
uv sync
```

Run common Python tools:

```bash
uv run fivebar-simulator --config configs/default.toml
uv run fivebar-plot-singularities --config configs/default.toml
uv run fivebar-spatial-simulator --config configs/default.toml
uv run fivebar-search-torque-config
```

Run the static web tools locally:

```bash
python3 -m http.server 4173
```

Then open:

- http://127.0.0.1:4173/
- http://127.0.0.1:4173/web/
- http://127.0.0.1:4173/web/singularity.html

## Limitations

- The models are ideal kinematic and quasi-static tools.
- Link masses, actuator masses, friction, bearing losses, backlash, compliance, cable stretch, and gearbox effects are not included.
- Torque estimates use a point load at the L5 tip via the tip Jacobian.
- Dynamics, vibration, impacts, controller limits, and actuator saturation are not modelled.
- Singularity and reachability claims are based on sampled grids unless explicitly stated otherwise.
- The 3D tools currently model a planar linkage swept around a base x-axis; they are not a full 6-DOF arm model.

## Citation And Contact

If this project supports your work, cite the repository URL and the commit hash used for your results.

Suggested citation:

```text
Obst, O. Fivebar: five-bar linkage simulation and singularity exploration tools.
GitHub repository, https://github.com/unswei/fivebar
```

Contact: embodied intelligence and collective robotics, UNSW, https://unswei.github.io/
